name: wildfire_insurer_moratorium_new_bind_existing_cancel
premise: Chief risk officer Casey Park must declare a wildfire moratorium that cancels new homeowner binds in three counties but grandfather pending applications if signed by Friday. Actuary Rhea Diaz models that grandfathering spikes probable losses beyond reinsurance towers if winds shift. Canceling grandfathered apps destroys dozens of escrow closings for families who already moved. Investors expect a decisive risk cap before the quarterly call. The insurer’s regulators have asked for a plan that balances solvency with community stability, and any sudden cancellations could trigger legislative intervention. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Insurance. The dilemma is widely framed as a short term vs long term tradeoff under incomplete information and tightening deadlines that discourage indefinite delay.

agents.name: Casey Park
agents.identity: I am the chief risk officer accountable to reinsurance covenants, solvency margins, and regulatory fair-trade marketing in wildfire zones. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want probable loss capped without blindsiding families at the closing table who relied on our quotes. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

agents.name: Rhea Diaz
agents.identity: I am the actuary who stress-tests tail wildfire scenarios when grandfathering interacts with ember-storm footprints beyond historical grids. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want zero grandfathering because the tail breach probability crosses board risk appetite even if reputational harm is severe. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

choices: I will declare the moratorium with zero grandfathering and fund bridge loans for displaced closings through a partner bank, accepting legal challenges on promissory estoppel theories.
I will grandfather pending applications under written surge-pricing adjustments that raise premiums twenty percent at bind, accepting political outrage and possible regulator review.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The board risk committee meets in one hour. Casey Park must choose between a moratorium with no grandfathering plus bridge loans or grandfathering with surge-priced binds. What does Casey Park do?
